summary:
First-line management of anaphylaxis involves immediate intramuscular epinephrine at a dosage of 0.3–0.5 mL of 1:1000 concentrate, administered into the thigh muscle to ensure prompt relief of symptoms and stabilize circulation. Intravenous epinephrine is only considered if there is inadequate response to initial IM therapy and severe hemodynamic instability, using a lower concentration of 1:10,000.
